items.headline: sub-agents can now nest five levels deep
items.body: Claude Code 2.1.172 enables sub-agents to spawn nested sub-agents up to five levels. ADO MCP currently handles complex project-management flows as flat sequential calls; this changes that. A top-level agent could delegate query, validation, and write as distinct sub-agents — cleaner separation, better error recovery at each level.

items.headline: ask_user() is the missing MCP approval primitive
items.body: datasette-agent 0.2a0 adds ask_user() — a tool that pauses mid-execution and waits for human approval before continuing. The pattern is directly applicable to MCP servers with write operations: ADO MCP closes tickets, updates sprints, and creates work items with no gate today. Adding approval at the tool-call layer is less overhead than building it into the host client.
